name: Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — running from Luna
name_original: Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — running desde Luna
region_intro: Jogging routes around Luna traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains, offering diverse terrain for runners. The region features varied elevations, with some routes including moderate climbs and descents. Natural features include open fields, river valleys, and areas with unique rock formations. The environment provides opportunities for outdoor activity amidst a mix of cultivated land and natural settings. Best jogging routes around Luna The most popular jogging route is Aguarales de Valdemilaz Loop, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route features gentle ascents through open terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Sanctuary of Monlora Loop, a moderate 4.2 miles (6.7 km) path. This route includes a notable climb to the Sanctuary of Monlora, offering expansive views. Local runners also love the Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — running from Luna, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Jogging around Luna is defined by open plains, gentle hills, and rural paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more challenging, longer routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore Luna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Luna?
A: There are currently &lt;strong&gt;4&lt;/strong&gt; dedicated running routes around Luna listed on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences for joggers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails near Luna?
A: Jogging routes around Luna traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains. You'll find diverse terrain with varied elevations, including moderate climbs and descents. Natural features include open fields, river valleys, and areas with unique rock formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the running routes in Luna?
A: The running routes in Luna cater to different abilities. There are &lt;strong&gt;3 moderate&lt;/strong&gt; routes and &lt;strong&gt;1 difficult&lt;/strong&gt; route available. This variety ensures options for both casual runners and those seeking a more challenging workout.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Luna?
A: Yes, several routes in Luna are circular, offering convenient start and end points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983266031/aguarales-de-valdemilaz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983632191/sanctuary-of-monlora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora Loop&lt;/a&gt; also provides a scenic circular path.

Q: What natural sights can I see while running in Luna?
A: While running in Luna, you can encounter impressive natural monuments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt;, with its unique geological formations, is a notable sight. You might also spot the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, which are towering conglomerate rock formations.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Luna?
A: The komoot community highly appreciates the varied terrain and natural beauty of Luna. With over 30 runners having explored the area, many enjoy the mix of open plains, gentle hills, and rural paths, as well as the opportunities to see unique rock formations and expansive views, such as those from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983632191/sanctuary-of-monlora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there longer running trails for experienced runners in Luna?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983261718/castillos-de-obano-y-yequera-running-from-luna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — running from Luna&lt;/a&gt; offers an 11.2-mile (18.0 km) difficult trail through varied rural landscapes, typically completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer historical or cultural points of interest?
A: While the primary focus is on natural landscapes, some routes may pass near historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983632191/sanctuary-of-monlora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes a climb to the Sanctuary, which offers both expansive views and a cultural landmark.

Q: What is a good running route for someone looking for moderate exercise in Luna?
A: A great option for moderate exercise is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983266031/aguarales-de-valdemilaz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This 6.3-mile (10.1 km) trail features gentle ascents through open terrain and takes approximately 1 hour 14 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic views in Luna?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer scenic views, particularly those that involve climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983632191/sanctuary-of-monlora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides expansive vistas from its highest point, showcasing the region's landscape of rolling hills and plains.

Q: Are there any specific geological features to look out for on the running trails?
A: Yes, the region is known for its unique geological formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt; are a must-see, featuring fascinating eroded earth pillars. Additionally, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, massive rock formations, are also nearby and can be seen from some routes or visited separately.
tips: Nice church located in the town of Luna. It is Romanesque in style and we will find it near the Torre Palacio del Reloj de la Corona from the 11th century, built as a watchtower for the lands reconquered from the Moors.

Obano Castle is an Aragonese fortification built in the second half of the 11th century, during the reign of Sancho Ramírez. It is located in the municipality of Luna, in the Cinco Villas region. This Romanesque-style castle was built to guard the communication routes between Huesca and Ejea de los Caballeros.

The castle is located near the Arba de Biel River and is known for its defensive tower, which rises to approximately 16 meters in height. Throughout its history, the castle has belonged to the Knights Templar and the Monastery of San Juan de la Peña.

Yéquera Castle, also known as Yecra Castle or Lacorvilla Castle, is located in the uninhabited medieval village of Yéquera. Its origins date back to a 10th-century Muslim fortification. In 1093, the Aragonese king Sancho Ramírez ordered the construction of a fortification to secure control of the newly conquered lands.
